Interested in learning more about, The Smarter Web Company, the UK’s largest Bitcoin treasury company?
 
Since our IPO in April 2025, our CEO, @asjwebley , has provided a weekly update to stakeholders  demonstrating a consistent and transparent approach to communication.  

In this week’s update, he shares his views on leverage / amplification, alongside other company specific news.  

Follow him for regular insights and to stay updated on our progress. 🇬🇧

LSE: #SWC | OTCQB: $TSWCF | FRA: $3M8

text:
My favourite type of RNS this morning - more orange.

The Smarter Web Company have purchased another 10₿, bringing total holdings to 2,840₿.

This marks our second Bitcoin purchase announcement this week, having added 25₿ on Tuesday.

The QTD Bitcoin yield is now 14.26%. One of our primary metrics remains long-term Bitcoin per share growth, and all capital market decisions continue to be made through that lens. We always encourage shareholders to assess us based on that metric and execution over time, rather than any single transaction in isolation.

The purchase was financed through our Coinbase facility. Leverage is now 10.89% (£16.5m), which we continue to monitor closely in line with market conditions. Our view remains that the market is rewarding Bitcoin treasury companies able to demonstrate a responsible and manageable level of leverage / amplification, which we believe we are approaching prudently.

We’ve now acquired 176 Bitcoin in 2026 - approximately 1.3₿ per day.

We continue to strengthen our position as the UK market leader. Scale and liquidity matter, and we remain laser-focused on continuing to build both.

Nothing makes the team happier than delivering more orange and we’re working around the clock to keep doing so.

LSE: #SWC | OTCQB: $TSWCF | FRA: $3M8

text:
This morning @smarterwebuk added 25₿  to our treasury, bringing total holdings to 2,830₿, with a 13.86% Bitcoin Yield QTD.  

The purchase was funded by a mixture of cash and debt from our coinbase facility. Our leverage ratio now stands at ~10.3% and we continue to believe that having a responsible level of leverage on the balance sheet makes sense at this point in the cycle.

Our CEO, @asjwebley shares his views on today’s purchase, how to evaluate our treasury strategy, and why individual transactions should not be judged in isolation. 

As we continue to scale, our aim remains simplicity in structure, although the operational management of the treasury will naturally become more complex over time - that is simply the reality of scaling a public company. 

Every decision we make is guided by what we believe will deliver the greatest long term benefit for shareholders, with management remaining focused on disciplined long term execution.

LSE: #SWC | OTCQB: $TSWCF | FRA: $3M8

text:
Enjoyed representing the @smarterwebuk at @MadBitcoin_2026.

I discussed how Bitcoin on the balance sheet can create strategic growth opportunities for publicly listed businesses, particularly through the ability to use equity as a form of acquisition currency.

A good example of this was our recent acquisition of Squarebird, another web design and digital marketing firm. 

As part of our stated acquisition strategy, we identified a business with strong economics and strategic fit, then used roughly 1% of the balance sheet value to complete a transaction that increased group revenues by approximately 10x.

Bitcoin is not just an asset for preserving value. For a public company, it can also become a strategic tool for compounding shareholder value.

LSE: #SWC | OTCQB: $TSWCF | FRA: $3M8

text:
Just arrived @MadBitcoin_2026. 

RNS today announcing gross proceeds of ~£0.9m from our ATM last week.

This was made possible by two factors

1. Our stock trading above 1.0x mNAV which was supported by the Bitcoin price but also our recent decision to introduce a level of responsible leverage (currently ~9.8%). We continue to believe the companies who are able to intelligently incorporate (and manage) amplification/leverage over time will be best positioned to sustain a premium to mNAV. 

2. Liquidity in our shares. Liquidity is one of the most important foundations of any successful public market strategy. Without sufficient liquidity, it becomes materially harder to efficiently raise capital, attract larger pools of institutional investors and support long term market participation. SWC LN is supported and traded across multiple platforms, brokers, market makers and investment banks. We continue working hard to improve liquidity, accessibility and market visibility across our UK, US and EU line.

LSE: #SWC | OTCQB: $TSWCF | FRA: $3M8

text:
Hi @kakapo_coder , appreciate the message and you’re right to highlight the nuance there.

As you correctly point out, FTSE weighting mechanics are more complex than that and are influenced by a range of factors including index composition changes, free float adjustments, issuance events and movements between index bands. As such, a higher share price / market cap alone doesn't automatically result in passive buying flows at the rebalance. 

The broader point we are trying to make is more around the medium and long term impact of size and visibility. As companies grow in market cap and liquidity within the FTSE, they can naturally become more relevant across a wider range of institutional, benchmarked and passive mandates over time (as well as custom / thematic ETF baskets).

We’ve publicly stated that one of our ambitions is to become a FTSE 250 and FTSE 100 company over our 10-year plan (not to be taken as a forecast), so these rebalance periods are useful milestones from an awareness and visibility perspective as the business continues to develop.

Appreciate you taking the time to comment - it's helpful and an important distinction to make.

text:
One of the key takeaways from the recent @Strategy  earnings call was the clear focus on simplifying and cleaning up the capital structure.

The broader message seemed to be that the various instruments issued over the last few years were part of a natural evolution as they built and proved out the Bitcoin treasury model; coverts, different forms of preferred equity etc.

Each served a purpose at a particular stage of the journey. But as the model matures, the direction of travel appears increasingly focused on simplification. The impression was that Strategy increasingly wants the market to view the business through a much simpler lens: 

- Common equity
- Perpetual preferred equity / digital credit instruments             
- Bitcoin

A cleaner structure with fewer moving parts and less complexity around maturities and refinancing cycles.

Overall, it feels like there is an increasing emphasis on building a capital structure that is simpler, with fewer moving parts.

At Smarter Web, we’ve spent the last few months continuing to simplify and strengthen our capital structure as we prepare for the next phase of our 10-year plan and to solidify our position as the UK's largest Bitcoin Treasury company. 

That has included reducing complexity through actions such as the pre-IPO warrant buybacks, improving flexibility through initiatives like the Coinbase lending facility, and focusing on building a structure that we believe is cleaner, more scalable and easier for both existing and prospective investors to understand over the long term.

LSE: #SWC | OTCQB: $TSWCF | FRA: $3M8

@Oliverhewett3 has been a supporter of The Smarter Web Company for a long time and now plays an important role helping strengthen the financial and operational foundations of the Company as we continue executing at a very pivotal stage of our 10-year plan.

text:
Hi Chirag, thanks for the question.  

You’re right that our current leverage ratio (~9.7%) is lower than some peers such as MSTR and ASST. The key difference is timing and approach. Those companies began introducing amplification earlier (in 2025), whereas we recently updated our Bitcoin treasury policy to allow for the gradual introduction of responsible leverage (following feedback from investors and the market). 

Since then, we’ve moved from ~0.4% in mid-March to ~9.7% today, which reflects a responsible and measured increase rather than a single step change.  

We don’t operate to a fixed leverage target. Instead, we assess market conditions, cost of capital, and overall balance sheet strength to determine how that evolves over time. It’s also worth noting that we are operating in a different regulatory environment and at a different stage of our growth.  

Our CEO, Andrew, has also shared some updated thinking on this in his recent weekly notes, which I’ve included below.  

Hope that helps, Jamie

xcancel.com/asjwebley/status/20504…

text:
GM - another step forward for our Bitcoin treasury at The Smarter Web Company.

Today’s RNS confirms we’ve acquired a further 27₿ (~£1.5m), taking total holdings to 2,805₿.

That’s now 141₿ added in 2026 across 8 purchases, with today’s increase bringing our QTD yield to 13.98%.

We also raised ~£0.1m via our ATM facility last week.

This purchase was funded through our Coinbase facility, with £15m currently drawn (up from £13.5m last week) and a leverage ratio of ~9.7%. The facility remains robust and flexible, with a 6.75–7.25% variable rate, no fixed term, and repayable at our discretion with no extra charges.

As Bitcoin appreciates, the value of our underlying assets increases, which naturally reduces our leverage ratio over time. This dynamic strengthens the balance sheet and provides more options to manage capital and support future growth.

We do not operate to a fixed leverage target. However, with Bitcoin still ~35% below recent all-time highs, we believe this is an appropriate step at this stage of the cycle. As the sector evolves, disciplined use of leverage (or amplification) is becoming increasingly important in sustaining premiums to mNAV and supporting long-term growth. As always, we will continue to monitor market conditions closely, with any further increases introduced in a measured and responsible manner.

Our balance sheet now holds approximately £165m ($215m) of pristine collateral. This scale supports the continued execution of our 10-year plan and positions us to pursue a broad range of opportunities as they arise.

LSE: #SWC | OTCQB: $TSWCF | FRA: $3M8

text:
April was another month of execution for The Smarter Web Company.

During the month, we:

🟠 Added 83₿, taking total holdings to 2,778₿
🟠 Produced a QTD BTC Yield of 12.98%
🟠 Introduced responsible debt/leverage as part of our Bitcoin acquisition strategy (current ratio ~ 9.1%)
🟠 Decreased the amount of Pre-IPO warrants by 39m warrants, significantly reducing the perceived overhang
🟠 Raised £1.5m gross through our ATM
🟠 Grew the team with the appointment of Oliver Hewett as Group Financial Controller
🟠 Granted LTIP awards aligned to long-term shareholder value creation
🟠 Celebrated our 1-year anniversary as a public listed company

The team is working hard across a range of initiatives as we continue to build the foundations for the next phase of our 10-Year Plan.

LSE: #SWC | OTCQB: $TSWCF | FRA: $3M8
